name: Business Cycles, 1903–1939 (Ayres Index)

book_quotes.quote: Figures 2.4A–C are monthly indicators of the cyclical component of business activity, compiled by the Cleveland Trust Company (Ayres 1939, table 9, appendix A, col. 1).
Figure 2.4A Business Cycles, 1831–1866. Figure 2.4B Business Cycles, 1867–1902. Figure 2.4C Business Cycles, 1903–1939. From Ayres (1939).

primary_source.publication: Ayres, L. P. (1939). Turning Points in Business Cycles. New York: Macmillan.
primary_source.units: Percent deviation from trend (cyclical component of business activity)
primary_source.replaced_by: No modern continuation; NBER macrohistory + BCDB business-cycle dates are the closest analogues.

concerns: See S204; Ayres index has no modern successor.

methodology_notes: Direct port of Ayres (1939) monthly series, 1903–1939 subperiod (Fig 2.4C). Highlights WWI and the 1929–1939 Great Depression.
